verses.commentary: Afterward Jesus appeared again to the disciples, by the Sea of Galilee. It happened this way. Third appearance (to group). Location: Galilee. Sea of Tiberias. Return to home territory. Epilogue begins.
Peter, Thomas, Nathanael, James and John (Zebedee's sons), two unnamed. Small group.

I'm going out to fish. I'll go with you. So they went out and got into the boat, but that night they caught nothing. Peter's initiative. Return to old profession. Others join. Night fishing. No catch. Futility. Back to beginning.
Jesus on shore. Unrecognized again. Distance or resurrection body.
He called out to them, Friends, haven't you any fish? No. Paidia (παιδία), children, lads. Friendly address. Fish question. Negative answer. Admission of failure.

He said, Throw your net on the right side of the boat and you will find some. When they did, they were unable to haul the net in because of the large number of fish. Instruction given. Right side. Obedience. Immediate abundance. Cannot pull in. Overwhelming catch. Luke 5 parallel.

Then the disciple whom Jesus loved said to Peter, It is the Lord! As soon as Simon Peter heard him say, It is the Lord, he wrapped his outer garment around him (for he had taken it off) and jumped into the water. John recognizes first. It is the Lord! Peter's impulsive response. Wraps garment. Dives in. Eagerness. Can't wait for boat.
Net towed. Two hundred cubits: about 90 meters. Near shore. Heavy load.
Anthrakia (ἀνθρακιά). Fish already cooking. Bread provided. Jesus prepares meal. Provision and hospitality.
Jesus said to them, Bring some of the fish you have just caught. Requests their catch. Their labor valued. Participation invited.
Specific count. Large fish. Net intact. Symbolism debated. Completeness? Gentile nations? Unbroken net: unity preserved despite abundance.

Jesus said to them, Come and have breakfast. None of the disciples dared ask Him, Who are You? They knew it was the Lord. Invitation to breakfast. Aristaō (ἀριστάω), eat breakfast. No one asks identity. They know. Certainty without questioning.
Took, gave. Feeding miracle echoes. Host serves. Provision continues.
Individual appearances not counted. Resurrection established. Multiple witnesses.

When they had finished eating, Jesus said to Simon Peter, Simon son of John, do you love Me more than these? Yes, Lord, he said, You know that I love You. Jesus said, Feed My lambs. After meal. Peter addressed. Full name: Simon son of John. Question: Agapaō (ἀγαπάω), supreme love. More than these: other disciples or fishing. Peter responds: Phileō (φιλέω), affection. You know: appeal to Jesus' knowledge. Command: Feed lambs. Pastoral commission.

Again Jesus said, Simon son of John, do you love Me? He answered, Yes, Lord, You know that I love You. Jesus said, Take care of My sheep. Second question. Agapaō again. Same response: Phileō. Command: Tend sheep. Poimainō (ποιμαίνω), shepherd. Leadership role.

The third time He said to him, Simon son of John, do you love Me? Peter was hurt because Jesus asked him the third time, Do you love Me? He said, Lord, You know all things; You know that I love You. Jesus said, Feed My sheep. Third question. This time Phileō. Jesus meets Peter's word. Peter hurt. Three questions reverse three denials. You know all things: omniscience acknowledged. Third command: Feed sheep. Full restoration. Triple commissioning.

Very truly I tell you, when you were younger you dressed yourself and went where you wanted; but when you are old you will stretch out your hands, and someone else will dress you and lead you where you do not want to go. Prophecy of martyrdom. Youth: independence. Old age: dependence. Stretch out hands: crucifixion. Led: arrested. Against will: execution.

Jesus said this to indicate the kind of death by which Peter would glorify God. Then He said to him, Follow Me! John's explanation. Death glorifies God. Crucifixion prophesied. Follow Me: command repeated (1:43). Discipleship unto death. Peter will follow Jesus to cross.

Peter turned and saw that the disciple whom Jesus loved was following them. (This was the one who had leaned back against Jesus at the supper and had said, Lord, who is going to betray You?) Peter turns. John following. Identification: beloved disciple. Reference to 13:23-25. Last Supper intimacy recalled.
When Peter saw him, he asked, Lord, what about him? Peter's question. Comparative curiosity. What about John? Concern for another's fate.

Jesus answered, If I want him to remain alive until I return, what is that to you? You must follow Me. Hypothetical statement. If I want him to remain. What to you? Not your concern. Command: You follow Me. Personal obedience. Don't compare. Focus on own calling.

Because of this, the rumor spread among the believers that this disciple would not die. But Jesus did not say that he would not die; He only said, If I want him to remain alive until I return, what is that to you? Misunderstanding. Rumor: John won't die. John corrects. Jesus didn't say that. Hypothetical, not promise. Clarification needed.
Beloved disciple wrote. Eyewitness testimony. We: editorial plural or church attestation. True testimony certified.
Many more acts. Selective record. World couldn't contain books. Vastness of Jesus' works. Gospel is sufficient, not exhaustive. Purpose achieved in what's written.
